T, 12018/1/2006-Fdre=]) 3. o, fey, Su gl -- 3 of 6 -- S THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [Part I—Skc. 3(D)] MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT AND FORESTS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 30th August, 2006 G.S.R.527(E)—In exercise of the powers conferred by clause (e) of sub-section 2 of section 63 of the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974, (6 of 1974) the Central Government hereby makes the following . rules for regulating the terms and conditions of service of the Chairman in the Central Poliution Control Board namely:- 1. Short title and commencement:-(1) These rules may be called the Chairman Central Pollution Control Board Recruitment Rules, 2006. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. The name of the post, number, classification and scale of pay attached thereto shall be as specified in columns (1) to (3) of the Schedule attached to these rules. 3. Pay, aliowances, leave and other conditions of service of Chairman Central Pollution Control Board - (1) The scale of pay of Chairman shall be as specified in column (4) of Schedule. (2) The rules and orders for the time being in force applicable to an officer holding posts of the corresponding scale of pay under the Central Government, shall regulate the conditions of service in respect of allowance, leave, joining time, joining time pay, provident fund, gratuity, age of superannuation, retirement benefits, medical facilities and other conditions of service of the Chairman of the Board. 4. Method of recruitment, age limits and other matters relating thereto shail be as specified in columns 5 to 14 of the said Schedule. 5. Disqualification:- No Person, (a) who has entered into or contracted a marriage with a person having a spouse living, or (b) who having a spouse living has entered into or contracted a marriage with any person, shall be eligible for appointment to the said post. Provided that the Central Government may, if satisfied that such marriage s permissible under personal law applicable to such person or the other party to the marriage and that there are other grounds for so doing, exempt any person from the operation of this rule. 6. Power to relax: - Where the Central Government is of the opinion that it is necessary or expedient to do so, it may by order for reasons to be recorded in writing, relax any of the provisions of these rules with respect to any class or category of persons. 7 Saving:- Nothing in these rules shall affect reservation, relaxation of age fimit and other concessions required to be provided for Schedule Caste/Schedule Tribe/Other Backward Classes/Ex-Servicemen and other special categories of persons in accordance with the orders issues by the Central Government from time to time in this regard. -- 4 of 6 -- o of service admissible under rule 30 of the Central Civil Services {Pension) Rules, 1972 s —ue33) ] I 6] TSI 2 STHERY SCHEDULE Name of the post No. of Classification Scale of pay Whether select-ionposts post or non- sefection post 1 2 3 4 5 Chaiman, Central One* General Central (Revised) Selection Poliution Control Service-Group-A Rs.22,400-525- Board Gazetted Non- 24,500 Ministerial * (2006) subject to variation dependent on workload Whether beneftt of added years | Age limit for direct recruits Educational and other quaifications required for direct recruits 6 Not applicable Not applicable.
